name: Inscribed Square Theorem (Toeplitz' Theorem)

phase_0.boundary_condition: the closed Jordan curve itself — topologically invariant

VINIT.justification: before any square is inscribed, no vertices exist; void state of the theorem

TANCH.justification: the closed boundary that contains and defines the entire system; topologically invariant closure

AFWD.justification: forward morphism: moving vertices along C toward equilibrium configuration

AREV.chosen_element: reversal of deformation; backtracking to prior vertex state
AREV.justification: contravariant descent: undoing a configuration step, parity flip in orientation

CLINK.chosen_element: composition of four vertex placements into a single square
CLINK.justification: sequential chaining: v1 → v2 → v3 → v4 → closed square; morphism composition

IMSCRIB.justification: self-reference: the square recognizes itself as the fixed point of the inscription process

FSPLIT.chosen_element: branching into two cases: orthogonal vs. non-orthogonal vertex pairs
FSPLIT.justification: co-multiplication: one continuous curve splits into two topological branches — (v1,v3) perpendicular vs. (v1,v3) non-perpendicular

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstitution of the original curve from both branches
FFUSE.justification: multiplication: both orthogonal and non-orthogonal configurations fuse back to the same Jordan curve C; FFUSE(FSPLIT(C)) = C

EVALT.chosen_element: orthogonal vertex pair found (right angle confirmed)
EVALT.justification: affirmative evaluation: the T-branch succeeds when opposite vertices form a 90° angle

EVALF.justification: negative evaluation: the F-branch records configurations where the angle constraint is not yet satisfied

ENGAGR.chosen_element: degenerate case: curve admits multiple inscribed squares simultaneously
ENGAGR.justification: paradice: both orthogonal and non-orthogonal solutions held without resolution; e.g., circle admits infinitely many inscribed squares

IFIX.chosen_element: permanent record of each inscribed square vertex
IFIX.justification: ROM fixation: once a vertex is placed on C and verified, it becomes an immutable record in the proof witness

phase_2.split_element: FSPLIT: branching into orthogonal vs. non-orthogonal vertex configurations
phase_2.split_input: the Jordan curve C with four vertices v1, v2, v3, v4 on it
split_outputs: T-branch: configurations where (v1,v3) and (v2,v4) are perpendicular diagonals
F-branch: configurations where the angle between diagonals is not 90°
phase_2.fuse_element: FFUSE: reconstituting the original curve from both branches
phase_2.fuse_result: the same Jordan curve C, now with the inscribed square proven to exist on it

phase_3.void_description: 00 — no square inscribed yet; configuration space empty; theorem unexecuted
phase_3.true_description: 01 — orthogonal square found; all four vertices on C with right angles verified; theorem satisfied
phase_3.false_description: 10 — current vertex configuration does not form a square; angle constraint violated; search continues
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice: the curve admits multiple inscribed squares simultaneously (e.g., circle, square, regular polygons); both solutions held without choosing

steps.domain_action: initialize empty configuration — no vertices placed on C yet
anchor the Jordan curve C as the immutable boundary containing all vertices
begin continuous deformation — move first vertex v1 along C in forward direction
compose v1 placement with curve topology — v1 is now bound to C
record v1 position permanently in the proof witness
move second vertex v2 along C, forward from v1
compose v2 placement with v1 and curve — v2 now bound to C
record v2 position permanently
move third vertex v3 along C, forward from v2
compose v3 placement — v3 now bound to C
record v3 position permanently
move fourth vertex v4 along C, forward from v3
compose v4 placement — v4 now bound to C, completing the quadrilateral
record v4 position permanently
branch into two cases — check if (v1,v3) and (v2,v4) are perpendicular diagonals
T-branch — test orthogonality: angle between diagonals = 90°?
if T-branch succeeds, the square recognizes itself as the fixed point of inscription
record the inscribed square configuration as permanent theorem witness
T-branch fuses back — orthogonal square reconstitutes the original curve C
F-branch — angle ≠ 90°; configuration does not form a square yet
F-branch reversal — backtrack v4 position, descend to prior state
F-branch forward — try next position for v4 along C
recompose v4 with new position
record new v4 attempt
re-branch — check orthogonality again with new v4
if orthogonal now, T-branch succeeds
square recognizes itself
record success
T-branch fuses back to C
if still non-orthogonal, F-branch continues iteration
backtrack v3, descend to prior state
try next position for v3
recompose v3
record v3 attempt
move v4 forward again from new v3
recompose v4
record v4 attempt
re-branch — check orthogonality
if orthogonal, T-branch succeeds
square recognizes itself
record success
T-branch fuses back to C
paradice case — if curve admits multiple inscribed squares (e.g., circle), both orthogonal and non-orthogonal solutions held simultaneously without resolution
all inscribed squares recognize themselves as fixed points
record all solutions as permanent witnesses
all branches fuse back to the same Jordan curve C
close the system — the theorem is complete; C contains at least one inscribed square

phase_5.compiler_frontend: topological continuity constraint solver — translates the requirement 'inscribe a square on C' into vertex placement and orthogonality checks
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: vertex-to-vertex adjacency messaging — each vertex communicates its position and angle to neighbors; diagonal orthogonality broadcast
phase_5.memory_mechanism: configuration state store — maintains current positions of v1, v2, v3, v4 on C; stores angle measurements; witness log of all attempts
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: continuous deformation scheduler — iterates vertex positions along C in order (v1 → v2 → v3 → v4 → check → backtrack if needed)
phase_5.alfs_store: the Jordan curve C itself — canonical reference; all vertices must lie on C; topological invariant bootstrap

phase_6.cycle_cost: each vertex placement and angle check consumes one unit of topological work; each backtrack reverses one unit
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Jordan curve C with no inscribed square; configuration space unexplored
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Jordan curve C with at least one inscribed square found and recorded; configuration space partially explored and fixed
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the theorem is entropy-conservative: the total topological information (curve C + inscribed square) is preserved through the deformation cycle; FFUSE(FSPLIT(C)) = C ensures no information is lost; the square is a fixed point of the continuous deformation, not a dissipative process

  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑭 𐑱)  -- [5] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (move second vertex v2 along C, forward from v1)

  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑾)  -- [7] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record v2 position permanently)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑭 𐑱)  -- [8] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (move third vertex v3 along C, forward from v2)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑭)  -- [9] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose v3 placement — v3 now bound to C)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑾)  -- [10] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record v3 position permanently)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑭 𐑱)  -- [11] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (move fourth vertex v4 along C, forward from v3)

  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑳 𐑭)  -- [43] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (all inscribed squares recognize themselves as fixed points)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑠 𐑙)  -- [44] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (record all solutions as permanent witnesses)
  (.arrow 𐑙 𐑭 𐑡)  -- [45] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 | fuse μ — assembly mode (all branches fuse back to the same Jordan curve C)

-- truth arm
noncomputable def inscribed_square_theorem_toeplitz_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (inscribed_square_theorem_toeplitz_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT
